title: Replace 2D wave mode with a 3D secondary mode
overview: Replace the existing 2D WaveField geometry system (horizontal sine waves rendered as THREE.Line objects) with a new 3D RibbonField geometry system — a 3D ribbon of points that maintains the point-cloud aesthetic while providing strong visual contrast to the existing PointCloud mode. The new mode uses THREE.Points with GPU shaders for audio-reactive deformation.

steps.title: Create RibbonField vertex shader
steps.description: Write a GLSL vertex shader for the ribbon point system. Accepts the same uniform set as pointWarp.vert.glsl (uTime, uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, uMotionAmplitude, uPointerDisturbance, uPointerPos, uPaletteHue, uPaletteSaturation, uCadence, uBreathScale, uBasePointSize, uNoiseFrequency, uRadialScale, uTwistStrength, uFieldSpread) plus per-vertex attributes (aHueOffset, aRandom). Bass drives ribbon-wide undulation and radial expansion. Treble drives per-point jitter and sparkle in lightness. Includes the same hsl2rgb and layeredNoise helpers from pointWarp. The key visual difference: points are distributed along twisted ribbon bands rather than a spherical shell.
details: Points arranged along ribbon curves that twist through 3D space
Bass energy expands ribbon width and adds macro sinusoidal displacement
Treble energy adds high-frequency per-point jitter and lightness sparkle
Pointer repulsion uses same screen-space approximation as pointCloud
Breathing scale and field spread uniforms apply identically
Motion amplitude gates all displacement magnitudes

steps.title: Create RibbonField geometry system
steps.description: Implement the GeometrySystem interface with init(), draw(), and cleanup(). On init, uses PRNG(seed + ':ribbon') to generate points distributed along 3-5 interleaving ribbon bands that spiral through 3D space. Each band is a parametric curve (helix or Lissajous-like) with points distributed along it and spread perpendicular to the curve tangent. Structure complexity controls ribbon count and lattice snapping. Density controls total point count (max ~1000). Uses THREE.Points with the ribbonWarp shader and additive blending.
details: Config interface: RibbonFieldConfig { maxPoints?: number; enableSparkle?: boolean }
Public interface: RibbonField extends GeometrySystem with pointCount and positions getters
Export createRibbonField(config?) factory function following same pattern as createPointCloud
Export getPointCount() and getPointPositions() helpers for testing
Point distribution: 3-5 ribbon bands (seeded), each a helical/spiral curve with points spread perpendicular to tangent
Buffer attributes: position(3), color(3), size(1), aHueOffset(1), aRandom(3)
ShaderMaterial with ribbonWarp shaders, additive blending, transparent, depthWrite false
draw() updates uniforms from frame.params (same pattern as pointCloud) plus mesh-level Y-axis drift rotation and Z-breathing
cleanup() removes mesh from scene, disposes geometry and material

steps.title: Add quality profile for ribbon mode
steps.description: Add maxRibbonPoints to QualityProfile interface and set per-tier values. Low: 200, Medium: 500, High: 1000. These mirror the maxPoints values but can diverge later if ribbon performance differs from point cloud.
details: Add maxRibbonPoints: number to QualityProfile interface
Low tier: 200, Medium tier: 500, High tier: 1000

steps.title: Replace WaveField with RibbonField in main.ts
steps.description: Replace the import of createWaveField with createRibbonField. Replace the waves instantiation with ribbonField using quality.maxRibbonPoints. Update the modeManager registration to use 'ribbon' instead of 'waves'. Remove the createWaveField import.
details: Remove: import { createWaveField } from './visual/systems/waveField'
Add: import { createRibbonField } from './visual/systems/ribbonField'
Replace waves creation with: const ribbon = createRibbonField({ maxPoints: quality.maxRibbonPoints, enableSparkle: quality.enableSparkle })
Replace mode entry: { name: 'ribbon', factory: () => ribbon }
Mode count stays at 3 (particles, ribbon, pointcloud) — mode selection and switching logic unchanged

steps.title: Create RibbonField fragment shader
steps.description: Reuse the same soft circular point rendering as pointWarp.frag.glsl — circular discard at radius 0.5, smoothstep falloff 0.3-0.5, alpha * 0.85. This can be identical to pointWarp.frag.glsl since both modes render points.
details: Same as pointWarp.frag.glsl — circular gl_PointCoord discard with soft edge

steps.title: Write RibbonField unit tests
steps.description: Port the WaveField test structure to test the RibbonField system. Cover: density-driven point count, deterministic seeding, different seeds produce different configs, draw does not throw with valid/edge-case params, boundary parameters, init adds THREE.Points to scene, cleanup removes and disposes, bass/treble/pointer influence uniforms, reduced motion, palette hue in colors, no localStorage/cookie access.
details: T-034-01: init creates points based on density and maxPoints config
T-034-02: same seed produces same initial point configuration
T-034-03: different seeds produce different configurations
T-034-04: draw does not throw with valid FrameState
T-034-05: draw does not throw with edge-case params (zero bass, zero treble, no pointer)
T-034-06: draw does not throw at boundary parameter values
T-034-07: init adds THREE.Points to scene (not THREE.Line)
T-034-08: points have position, color, size, aHueOffset, aRandom buffer attributes
T-034-09: draw updates shader uniforms including bassEnergy and trebleEnergy
T-034-10: bass and treble are mapped to distinct uniforms (uBassEnergy vs uTrebleEnergy)
T-034-11: cleanup removes mesh from scene and disposes geometry/material
T-034-12: no localStorage or cookie access during ribbon operations

steps.title: Update modeManager integration test
steps.description: Update the integration test T-026-24 that currently uses real ParticleField and WaveField to instead use ParticleField and RibbonField. This confirms mode switching works with the new real system.
details: Replace WaveField import with RibbonField import
Replace createWaveField() with createRibbonField()
Rename mode entry from 'waves' to 'ribbon'

steps.title: Remove WaveField files
steps.description: Delete the old waveField.ts source file and its test file tests/visual/systems/waveField.test.ts since the 2D wave mode is fully replaced.
details: Delete src/visual/systems/waveField.ts
Delete tests/visual/systems/waveField.test.ts

steps.title: Build verification and smoke test
steps.description: Run npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors or missing imports. Run the test suite to confirm all new and existing tests pass. Manually verify no console errors on load.
details: npm run build succeeds
All tests pass (vitest)
No references to waveField remain in source (except git history)
Mode manager still has 3 modes registered
No forbidden storage APIs used

acceptance_verification.second_3d_mode_implemented: RibbonField renders THREE.Points in 3D space (twisted ribbon bands) — not 2D canvas lines. Uses same GPU shader pipeline as PointCloud.
acceptance_verification.audio_reactive_bass_treble_distinct: Bass drives macro ribbon undulation and radial expansion via uBassEnergy uniform. Treble drives per-point jitter and lightness sparkle via uTrebleEnergy uniform. Verified by unit tests checking distinct uniform updates.
acceptance_verification.mode_selection_switching_seeded: No changes to modeManager.ts — selection uses createPRNG(seed + ':mode'), switch interval is seeded 90-180s. RibbonField is registered as a drop-in replacement in the modes array.
acceptance_verification.switching_preserves_audio: Audio pipeline runs in renderLoop.ts independent of geometry systems. Mode switch calls cleanup() then init() synchronously — no audio graph interaction. Verified by existing modeManager tests (T-026-21: all frames rendered across switches).

design_rationale: A 3D ribbon-of-points approach was chosen because: (1) it stays 'close to point clouds' as the ticket requests — same THREE.Points rendering, same shader structure, same additive blending aesthetic; (2) it provides strong visual contrast to the spherical-shell PointCloud — elongated, flowing bands vs compact sphere; (3) the helical/spiral distribution creates organic, evolving shapes that respond differently to bass (whole-ribbon sway) vs treble (per-point shimmer); (4) it reuses the existing shader uniform interface, making it a clean drop-in replacement with no render loop changes.
